description: Capture the validationforge plugin registration from the Claude Code installed-plugins registry (~/.claude/installed_plugins.json) and verify the on-disk path the registry points to exists.

manifest.name: validationforge
manifest.description: No-mock validation platform for Claude Code. Ship verified code, not 'it compiled' code.

observations: The registry at ~/.claude/installed_plugins.json contains exactly one entry keyed 'validationforge@validationforge'.
The 'path' field points to /Users/nick/.claude/plugins/cache/validationforge/validationforge/1.0.0, which exists on disk as a directory.
The plugin directory contains the expected top-level subtrees: .claude-plugin/, agents/, commands/, config/, docs/, hooks/, plans/, rules/, scripts/, skills/, templates/.
The manifest .claude-plugin/plugin.json declares name='validationforge' and version='1.0.0', matching the registry key suffix and the last path segment.

The installedAt timestamp (2026-04-09T02:30:17.034Z) aligns with the registry mtime (2026-04-08T22:30:17-0400 = 2026-04-09T02:30:17Z) within one second, confirming the entry was written atomically by the install step.
